conservation_statuses.description: Extremely small and fragmented population (estimated between 115 and 150 breeding pairs). Positive trend in numbers thanks to an action plan.

results.wikipedia_summary: The little owl (Athene noctua) is a bird that inhabits much of the temperate and warmer parts of Europe, Asia east to Korea, and north Africa. It was introduced into Britain at the end of the nineteenth century and into the South Island of New Zealand in the early twentieth century.
